info.title: JIM API
info.description: Programmatic access to JIM's identity lifecycle management capabilities. Use this API to configure Connected Systems, manage Synchronisation Rules, query the Metaverse, monitor Activities, and automate deployment via scripting or CI/CD pipelines.

get.description:
Browse files and directories within the JIM Container's allowed mount points.
Used when configuring file-based connectors (e.g. CSV) to select import/export paths.
Omit the path parameter to list the allowed root directories.
Only paths within the configured allowed roots are accessible; all other paths return 403.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The directory path to list. If not specified, returns the allowed root directories.

403.description: The requested path is outside allowed directories.

404.description: The specified directory does not exist.

get.description:
Returns the root directories that JIM is permitted to browse.
These correspond to Docker volume mounts configured for file-based connectors.

200.description: A list of allowed root directory paths.

get.description:
Check whether a given path falls within the allowed root directories.
Use this before attempting to configure a connector with a file path.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The path to validate.

200.description: True if the path is allowed, false otherwise.

get.description:
Verifies the application is ready by checking database connectivity
and that the service is not in maintenance mode.
Returns 503 Service Unavailable if the application is not ready.

Used by orchestrators to determine if the service needs to be restarted.
This check does not verify external dependencies like the database.

post.description:
Deletes expired CSO changes, MVO changes, and Activities older than the configured
retention period. The cleanup is limited by the configured batch size to prevent
long-running transactions. For large volumes, call this endpoint multiple times
or rely on automatic housekeeping cleanup.

This operation creates an Activity record to audit the cleanup.

200.description: Returns cleanup result with deletion statistics.

401.description: If the user is not authenticated.

get.summary: Get Change History count for a Connected System
get.description:
Returns the total number of Connected System Object (CSO) change records currently retained
for the specified Connected System. Each record represents a tracked change (create, update,
or delete) captured during synchronisation, and contributes to the audit trail used for
reconciliation, compliance reporting, and troubleshooting.

This count reflects records still within the configured history retention period; older
records are pruned automatically by housekeeping or via the cleanup endpoint. Use this
endpoint to gauge change volume per Connected System before triggering manual cleanup or
when monitoring synchronisation activity.

parameters.name: connectedSystemId
parameters.description: The Connected System ID.

200.description: Returns the count of change records.

404.description: If the Connected System is not found.

401.description: If the user is not authenticated.

get.description:
Returns the ordered list of import contributions to this Metaverse Attribute for a given Metaverse Object
Type (#91), highest priority first. Disabled Synchronisation Rules are included; they hold position but
never contribute during resolution. An empty list means the Attribute has no import contributors.

parameters.name: metaverseAttributeId
parameters.description: The Metaverse Attribute.

parameters.name: metaverseObjectTypeId
parameters.description: The Metaverse Object Type that scopes the priority list.

put.description:
Transactionally renumbers the priorities of all import contributions to this Metaverse Attribute for a
given Metaverse Object Type (#91), and applies each contribution's "Null is a value" flag. The request must
list every current contributing mapping for the Attribute exactly once, in the desired priority order
(highest first). To move a single mapping without restating the whole list, use the move endpoint instead.
Returns the resulting order.

parameters.name: metaverseAttributeId
parameters.description: The Metaverse Attribute.

parameters.name: metaverseObjectTypeId
parameters.description: The Metaverse Object Type that scopes the priority list.

requestBody.description: The contributors in the desired priority order.

200.description: Priority order updated successfully; returns the updated order.

400.description: The requested order does not match the Attribute's current contributor set.

401.description: User could not be identified from authentication token.

put.summary: Move a mapping to a new priority position
put.description:
Repositions a single contributing mapping to the given 1-based priority position for this Metaverse
Attribute on a given Metaverse Object Type (#91), shuffling the other contributors to keep the list
contiguous, then renumbering all affected rows in one transaction. The caller states only the new position;
the engine keeps the order gap-free and duplicate-free. Optionally also updates the moved mapping's "Null is
a value" flag. Returns the resulting order, so the caller never has to renumber siblings or re-fetch.

parameters.name: metaverseAttributeId
parameters.description: The Metaverse Attribute.

parameters.name: metaverseObjectTypeId
parameters.description: The Metaverse Object Type that scopes the priority list.

parameters.name: mappingId
parameters.description: The contributing mapping to move.

requestBody.description: The desired position (and optional "Null is a value" flag).

200.description: Mapping moved successfully; returns the resulting order.

400.description: The mapping is not a contributor to the Attribute.

401.description: User could not be identified from authentication token.

get.description:
The DisplayName Attribute is always included in the response. Use the `attributes` parameter
to request additional Attributes to be included. This follows a common pattern in APIs and
PowerShell modules where clients can specify which properties to retrieve.

Use `?attributes=*` to include all Attributes.

**Filtering:**
- `search` - Searches display name (partial match, case-insensitive)
- `filterAttributeName` + `filterAttributeValue` - Filters by specific Attribute (exact match, case-insensitive)

Examples:
- `?attributes=FirstName&attributes=LastName&attributes=Email` - Include specific Attributes
- `?attributes=*` - Include all Attributes
- `?filterAttributeName=Account Name&filterAttributeValue=jsmith` - Find by account name

post.description:
Wipes all data and configuration from the database, preserving the schema,
EF Core migration history, and the rows seeded at first launch (built-in metaverse
attributes and object types, built-in roles, built-in connector definitions,
built-in example data sets and templates, built-in predefined searches, the
singleton service settings record, and infrastructure API keys).

By default the Metaverse Objects holding the built-in Administrator role are preserved so the
operator is not locked out of the portal; set `includeAdministrators` to remove them too.
A Reset activity recording the initiating principal is always created, and every existing portal
session is invalidated (the authentication epoch is advanced).

Refuses with HTTP 409 if any activity is currently in progress, or if an administrator-inclusive
wipe is requested with no initial administrator configured and the lockout risk is not acknowledged.

**This is destructive and cannot be undone.** Callers should obtain user confirmation
before invoking, and take a database backup first.

requestBody.description: Reset options. An empty body performs the default (administrator-preserving) reset.

409.description: If activities are in progress, or the lockout guard refuses the wipe.

401.description: If the user is not authenticated.

403.description: If the user lacks the Administrator role.

totalExported.description: Number of objects exported to target systems (includes both initial creation and subsequent updates).

totalDeprovisioned.description: Number of objects deprovisioned from target systems.

totalPendingExports.description:
Number of Pending Exports staged for the next export run.
These are exports that were previously executed but not yet confirmed,
giving operators visibility into what changes will be made to Connected Systems.

totalPendingExportsConfirmed.description:
Number of Pending Exports that were fully confirmed and deleted.
The exported attribute values matched the imported values.

totalPendingExportsRetrying.description:
Number of Pending Exports with unconfirmed attributes that will be retried.
Some attribute values did not match; they will be re-exported on the next export run.

totalPendingExportsFailed.description:
Number of Pending Exports that failed after maximum retry attempts.
Manual intervention may be required to resolve these exports.

totalCreated.description: Number of MVOs created directly (data generation, admin UI) rather than via projection/sync.

description.description: Optional description of what this schedule does.

triggerType.description: Whether this schedule runs on a cron trigger or is manual-only.

cronExpression.description:
The cron expression for scheduled triggers.
This is typically generated from the pattern configuration fields, but can be provided directly when PatternType is Custom.

patternType.description: The type of schedule pattern: SpecificTimes, Interval, or Custom.

daysOfWeek.description:
Days of week to run (0=Sunday, 6=Saturday). Only used when PatternType != Custom.
Provide as comma-separated values: "1,2,3,4,5" for Mon-Fri.

runTimes.description:
Times to run when PatternType is SpecificTimes.
Provide as comma-separated 24h times: "09:00,12:00,15:00,18:00".

intervalValue.description: Interval value when PatternType is Interval (e.g., 2 for "every 2 hours").

oneOf.description: Interval unit when PatternType is Interval.

intervalWindowStart.description: Optional start time for interval window (e.g., "06:00"). Only used when PatternType is Interval.

intervalWindowEnd.description: Optional end time for interval window (e.g., "18:00"). Only used when PatternType is Interval.

isEnabled.description: Whether the schedule should be enabled upon creation.

steps.description: The steps to include in this schedule.

type.description: The logical operator for this group: "All" (AND) or "Any" (OR).

connectedSystemAttributeId.description:
The Connected System Attribute ID to evaluate (for import Synchronisation Rules).
Either MetaverseAttributeId or ConnectedSystemAttributeId must be provided.

comparisonType.description:
The comparison operator: Equals, NotEquals, Contains, StartsWith, EndsWith,
NotContains, NotStartsWith, NotEndsWith, LessThan, LessThanOrEquals, GreaterThan, GreaterThanOrEquals.

stringValue.description: The string value to compare against (for text attributes).

intValue.description: The integer value to compare against (for number attributes).

longValue.description: The long integer value to compare against (for long number attributes).

dateTimeValue.description: The date/time value to compare against (for datetime attributes).

boolValue.description: The boolean value to compare against (for boolean attributes).

guidValue.description: The GUID value to compare against (for GUID attributes).

caseSensitive.description:
When true (default), value comparisons are case-sensitive.
When false, comparisons ignore case differences.
Only applies to text/string comparisons.

CreateScopingCriterionRequest.description:
Request DTO for creating a new scoping criterion within a group.
For export Synchronisation Rules, provide MetaverseAttributeId.
For import Synchronisation Rules, provide ConnectedSystemAttributeId.

targetMetaverseAttributeId.description: For import rules: The target Metaverse Attribute ID.

targetConnectedSystemAttributeId.description: For export rules: The target Connected System Attribute ID.

oneOf.description:
For import rules only: inbound text value-processing transforms applied to the value as it flows to
the target Metaverse attribute (a comma-separated set of flag names, e.g. "TreatWhitespaceAsNoValue,
TrimWhitespace"). When omitted, defaults to TreatWhitespaceAsNoValue. Ignored for export rules.

For import rules only: case normalisation applied to the inbound text value (None, Upper, Lower or
Title). When omitted, defaults to None. Ignored for export rules.

nullIsValue.description:
For import rules only: when true, a connected, in-scope contribution of null/absent asserts "no value" and
stops attribute priority resolution falling through to lower-priority contributions ("Null is a value", #91).
When omitted, defaults to false. Ignored for export rules. Priority itself is not set at creation: a new
import mapping lands at the safe-addition default (lowest priority) and is ordered later via the
attribute-priority-order endpoint.

An expression to evaluate for this source.
Uses DynamicExpresso syntax with mv["AttributeName"] and cs["AttributeName"] for attribute access.
Example: "CN=" + EscapeDN(mv["Display Name"]) + ",OU=Users,DC=domain,DC=local"

intervalWindowEnd.description: Optional end time for interval window (e.g., "18:00"). Only used when PatternType is Interval.

isEnabled.description: Whether the schedule is currently enabled.

nextRunTime.description: When the schedule is next due to run (UTC).

stepCount.description: The number of steps in this schedule.

lastUpdated.description: When the schedule was last modified (UTC).

ScheduleExecutionDto.description: DTO for a schedule execution in list views.

name.description: The step name.

executionMode.description:
How this step executes relative to other steps at the same index.
Sequential means it runs alone; ParallelWithPrevious means it runs concurrently with siblings.

connectedSystemId.description:
The Connected System ID for RunProfile steps.
Useful for distinguishing parallel sub-steps at the same StepIndex.

status.description: The current status of this step.

taskId.description: The worker task ID if this step has been queued.

errorMessage.description: Error message if the step failed.

activityId.description: The Activity ID associated with this step's worker task (if available).

activityStatus.description:
The Activity status for this step (e.g. Complete, CompleteWithWarning, FailedWithError).
Only populated when the step has completed and an activity exists.

ScheduleExecutionStepDto.description: DTO for an individual step's status within an execution.

executionId.description: The execution ID for tracking the schedule run.

ScheduleRunResponse.description: Response returned when a schedule is triggered manually.

id.description: The unique identifier of the step.

stepIndex.description:
The order in which this step executes (0-based).
Steps with the same index run in parallel.

name.description:
Display name for the step. For RunProfile steps, this is null and the name should
be derived from the Connected System and Run Profile. For other step types, this
contains the user-specified name.

executionMode.description: How this step executes relative to the previous step.

stepType.description: The type of action this step performs (discriminator for configuration properties).

continueOnFailure.description: Whether to continue the schedule if this step fails.

timeoutSeconds.description: Optional timeout for this step in seconds.

connectedSystemId.description: The Connected System ID (for RunProfile steps).

runProfileId.description: The Run Profile ID (for RunProfile steps).

scriptPath.description: The path to the PowerShell script (for PowerShell steps).

executablePath.description: The path to the executable (for Executable steps).

arguments.description: Arguments to pass to the script or executable (for PowerShell and Executable steps).

sqlScriptPath.description: The path to the SQL script (for SqlScript steps).

ScheduleStepDto.description:
DTO for a schedule step with polymorphic configuration.
The step type determines which configuration properties are relevant.

id.description:
The unique identifier of the step. Use an existing ID to update,
or omit/use empty GUID to create a new step.

stepIndex.description:
The order in which this step executes (0-based).
Steps with the same index run in parallel.

name.description:
Display name for the step. Required for non-RunProfile steps (PowerShell, Executable, SqlScript).
For RunProfile steps, this should be null as the name is derived from the FKs.

executionMode.description: How this step executes relative to the previous step.

stepType.description: The type of action this step performs (discriminator for configuration properties).

continueOnFailure.description: Whether to continue the schedule if this step fails.

timeoutSeconds.description: Optional timeout for this step in seconds.

connectedSystemId.description: The Connected System ID (required for RunProfile steps).

runProfileId.description: The Run Profile ID (required for RunProfile steps).

scriptPath.description: The path to the PowerShell script (required for PowerShell steps).

executablePath.description: The path to the executable (required for Executable steps).

workingDirectory.description: The working directory (optional for Executable steps).

arguments.description: Arguments to pass to the script or executable (optional for PowerShell and Executable steps).

sqlConnectionString.description: The connection string (required for SqlScript steps).

sqlScriptPath.description: The path to the SQL script (required for SqlScript steps).

ScheduleStepRequest.description:
Request DTO for a schedule step with polymorphic configuration.
The step type determines which configuration properties are required.

key.description: Unique setting key using dot notation (e.g., "ChangeTracking.CsoChanges.Enabled").

description.description: Description of what this setting controls.

valueType.description: Data type of the setting value (e.g., "String", "Boolean", "Integer").

defaultValue.description: The default value as a string.

value.description: The current overridden value, or null if using the default.

effectiveValue.description: The effective value (Value if set, otherwise DefaultValue).

isReadOnly.description: Whether this setting is read-only (e.g., mirrored from environment variables).

isOverridden.description: Whether the current value differs from the default.

ServiceSettingDto.description: API representation of a service setting.

value.description:
The new value for the setting as a string. Set to null to clear the override
and revert to the default value.

ServiceSettingUpdateRequestDto.description: Request DTO for updating a service setting value.

SetAttributePriorityContributorRequest.description:
A single entry in a SetAttributePriorityOrderRequest: the mapping and its "Null is a value" flag.
The mapping's priority is derived from its position in the list (first = priority 1).

SetAttributePriorityOrderRequest.description:
Request DTO for setting an attribute's priority order (#91). The contributors must list every current
contributing mapping for the attribute exactly once, in the desired priority order (highest first); the
server renumbers all of them transactionally.

mode.description:
The new Object Matching Rule mode.
0 = ConnectedSystem (simple mode), 1 = SyncRule (advanced mode).

SwitchObjectMatchingModeRequest.description: Request DTO for switching the Object Matching Rule mode on a Connected System.

inboundValueProcessing.description:
Inbound (import) text value-processing transforms applied to the value as it flows to the target
Metaverse attribute. Only meaningful for import mappings targeting text attributes. Serialised as a
comma-separated set of flag names.

caseNormalisation.description:
Inbound (import) case normalisation applied to the text value. Only meaningful for import mappings
targeting text attributes.

priority.description:
Attribute priority for this import contribution (#91). Lower numbers win (1 is highest); int.MaxValue is
the safe-addition sentinel meaning "not yet ordered, never wins". Only meaningful for import mappings.

nullIsValue.description:
When true, a connected, in-scope contribution of null/absent for this attribute asserts "no value" and
stops resolution falling through to lower-priority contributions ("Null is a value", #91). Only meaningful
for import mappings.

expression.description:
The expression to evaluate for this source.
Uses DynamicExpresso syntax with mv["AttributeName"] and cs["AttributeName"] for attribute access.

id.description: The unique identifier of the scoping criteria.

metaverseAttributeId.description: The Metaverse Attribute ID being evaluated (for export Synchronisation Rules).

metaverseAttributeName.description: The name of the Metaverse Attribute being evaluated (for export Synchronisation Rules).

connectedSystemAttributeId.description: The Connected System Attribute ID being evaluated (for import Synchronisation Rules).

connectedSystemAttributeName.description: The name of the Connected System Attribute being evaluated (for import Synchronisation Rules).

attributeDataType.description: The data type of the attribute.

comparisonType.description: The comparison operator (Equals, NotEquals, Contains, StartsWith, etc.).

stringValue.description: The string value to compare against (for text attributes).

intValue.description: The integer value to compare against (for number attributes).

longValue.description: The long integer value to compare against (for long number attributes).

dateTimeValue.description: The date/time value to compare against (for datetime attributes).

boolValue.description: The boolean value to compare against (for boolean attributes).

guidValue.description: The GUID value to compare against (for GUID attributes).

caseSensitive.description:
When true (default), value comparisons are case-sensitive.
When false, comparisons ignore case differences.
Only applies to text/string comparisons.

SyncRuleScopingCriteriaDto.description: API representation of a Synchronisation Rule scoping criteria (a single filter condition).

id.description: The unique identifier of the criteria group.

type.description: The logical operator for this group: "All" (AND) or "Any" (OR).

position.description: The position/order of this group (for ordering at same level).

childGroups.description: Nested child groups (for complex logical expressions).

SyncRuleScopingCriteriaGroupDto.description: API representation of a Synchronisation Rule scoping criteria group (contains criteria with AND/OR logic).

includeAdministrators.description:
When false (the default), Metaverse Objects holding the built-in Administrator role are preserved
so the operator is not locked out of the portal. When true, those administrator identities are
removed as well, leaving a true brand-new install.

acknowledgeAdministratorLockout.description:
Acknowledges the portal lockout risk. An administrator-inclusive wipe is refused when no initial
administrator is configured (JIM_SSO_INITIAL_ADMIN), because the portal would then be inaccessible
afterwards. Set to true to proceed anyway (for example when access is retained via the
infrastructure API key). Ignored when IncludeAdministrators is false.

expression.description:
The expression to test.
Uses DynamicExpresso syntax with mv["AttributeName"] and cs["AttributeName"] for attribute access.

mvAttributes.description:
Sample Metaverse attribute values to use during evaluation.
Keys are attribute names, values are the attribute values.

csAttributes.description:
Sample Connected System attribute values to use during evaluation.
Keys are attribute names, values are the attribute values.

TestExpressionRequest.description: Request DTO for testing an expression with sample attribute data.

isValid.description: Indicates whether the expression is valid and evaluated successfully.

result.description: The result of evaluating the expression (if successful).

errorMessage.description: Error message if the expression is invalid or evaluation failed.

errorPosition.description: Position in the expression where an error occurred (if applicable).

TestExpressionResponse.description: Response DTO for expression test results.

TrustedCertificateDetailDto.description: Detailed API representation of a TrustedCertificate (excludes raw certificate bytes for security).

selected.description: Whether this attribute is selected for management by JIM.

isExternalId.description: Indicates if this attribute is a unique identifier for the object type in the Connected System.

isSecondaryExternalId.description: Indicates if this attribute is used as a secondary identifier by the Connected System (e.g., DN in LDAP).

selected.description: Whether this object type is selected for management by JIM.

removeContributedAttributesOnObsoletion.description:
Controls whether Metaverse Object attribute values contributed by a Connected System Object of this type
should be removed when the CSO is obsoleted.

selected.description:

name.description: The updated name for the Connected System.

description.description: The updated description for the Connected System.

settingValues.description: Updated setting values as key-value pairs where key is the setting ID.

maxExportParallelism.description:
Maximum number of export batches to process concurrently.
Only applicable when the connector supports parallel export.

changeReason.description: An optional reason for the change, recorded against this Connected System's change history.

name.description: The updated name for the Metaverse Attribute.

oneOf.description: The updated data type of the attribute.
Whether the attribute is single-valued or multi-valued.

objectTypeIds.description:
Optional list of object type IDs to associate this attribute with.
This will replace the existing associations.

UpdateMetaverseAttributeRequest.description: Request DTO for updating an existing Metaverse Attribute.

oneOf.description: Determines when Metaverse Objects of this type should be automatically deleted.

deletionGracePeriod.description:
Optional grace period before deletion is executed, as an ISO 8601 duration string (e.g., "00:01:00" for 1 minute, "1.00:00:00" for 1 day).
Set to "00:00:00", TimeSpan.Zero, or null for immediate deletion when conditions are met.

deletionTriggerConnectedSystemIds.description:
List of Connected System IDs that are authoritative sources for deletion.
Required when DeletionRule is WhenAuthoritativeSourceDisconnected.
When set: Delete MVO if ANY of these specific systems disconnect.
Ignored when DeletionRule is Manual or WhenLastConnectorDisconnected.

UpdateMetaverseObjectTypeRequest.description: Request DTO for updating a MetaverseObjectType's deletion rules.

order.description: The new evaluation order for this rule.

metaverseObjectTypeId.description: The new Metaverse Object Type ID to search when evaluating this rule.

targetMetaverseAttributeId.description: The new target Metaverse attribute ID.

sources.description: If specified, replaces all sources with these new sources.

caseSensitive.description:
When true, attribute value comparisons are case-sensitive.
When false, comparisons ignore case differences.

UpdateObjectMatchingRuleRequest.description: Request DTO for updating an Object Matching Rule.

isEnabled.description:
When set, toggles whether the search is visible to end users via the portal and the
search API. Disabled searches remain visible in the admin UI.

UpdatePredefinedSearchRequest.description:
Partial update payload for a predefined search. All fields are optional; only the fields that
are provided (non-null) will be applied.

name.description: The updated name for the Run Profile.

pageSize.description: The updated page size.

partitionId.description: Updated partition ID if the connector supports partitions.

filePath.description: Updated file path for file-based connectors.

name.description: The user-defined name for this schedule.

description.description: Optional description of what this schedule does.

triggerType.description: Whether this schedule runs on a cron trigger or is manual-only.

cronExpression.description:
The cron expression for scheduled triggers.
This is typically generated from the pattern configuration fields, but can be provided directly when PatternType is Custom.

patternType.description: The type of schedule pattern: SpecificTimes, Interval, or Custom.

daysOfWeek.description:
Days of week to run (0=Sunday, 6=Saturday). Only used when PatternType != Custom.
Provide as comma-separated values: "1,2,3,4,5" for Mon-Fri.

runTimes.description:
Times to run when PatternType is SpecificTimes.
Provide as comma-separated 24h times: "09:00,12:00,15:00,18:00".

intervalValue.description: Interval value when PatternType is Interval (e.g., 2 for "every 2 hours").

oneOf.description: Interval unit when PatternType is Interval.

intervalWindowStart.description: Optional start time for interval window (e.g., "06:00"). Only used when PatternType is Interval.

intervalWindowEnd.description: Optional end time for interval window (e.g., "18:00"). Only used when PatternType is Interval.

steps.description:
The complete list of steps for this schedule.
Existing steps not in this list will be deleted.

UpdateScheduleRequest.description: Request DTO for updating an existing schedule.

type.description: The logical operator for this group: "All" (AND) or "Any" (OR).

UpdateScopingCriteriaGroupRequest.description: Request DTO for updating a scoping criteria group.

name.description: The updated name for the Synchronisation Rule.

enabled.description: Whether the Synchronisation Rule is enabled.

projectToMetaverse.description: Whether this rule should also cause objects to be projected to the Metaverse (for Import rules).

provisionToConnectedSystem.description: Whether this rule should also cause objects to be provisioned to the Connected System (for Export rules).

enforceState.description:
For Export rules: When true (default), inbound changes from the target system will trigger
re-evaluation of this export rule to detect and remediate drift.
Only applicable when Direction = Export.

oneOf.description:
For Import rules: Action to take when a CSO falls out of this rule's scope.
Disconnect breaks the CSO -> MVO join; whether the attributes contributed by
this Connected System are also recalled from the MVO depends on the CSO type's
RemoveContributedAttributesOnObsoletion flag, the MVO type's deletion grace
period, and whether the MVO is slated for immediate deletion. RemainJoined
keeps the join intact and stops further Attribute Flow. Only applicable when
Direction = Import.

changeReason.description: An optional reason for the change, recorded against this Synchronisation Rule's change history.

UpdateSyncRuleRequest.description: Request DTO for updating an existing Synchronisation Rule.
